text.title: Python Type Hints Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Python typing rollouts. Each has a symptom, a root cause, and a fix the validator or CI gate can enforce.

antipattern.symptom: `from __future__ import annotations` added project-wide; FastAPI body parsing or Pydantic v1 models throw `Type 'X' is not defined` at runtime; type-check passes silently.

root-cause.source: FastAPI issue tracker; Pydantic v1 docs

antipattern.root-cause: `from __future__ import annotations` stringifies all annotations at runtime. Pydantic v1 and older FastAPI body-parameter resolution use runtime type inspection and break silently when annotations are strings.
antipattern.fix: Pin Pydantic v2 + FastAPI ≥0.100 if `from __future__ import annotations` is desired, or omit the future import on files that define Pydantic v1 models / FastAPI body params. CI grep: `grep -l 'from __future__ import annotations' $(grep -rl 'BaseModel\|@app\.\(get\|post\)')`.

antipattern.symptom: Files contain `# type: ignore` with no error code; new type bugs are masked because mypy can no longer surface them; `warn_unused_ignores` cannot fire because there's no code to compare.

root-cause.source: Internal — observed in test-author subagent runs Q1 2026

antipattern.root-cause: Under pressure from a mypy gate the agent reaches for the broadest silencer first. Bare `# type: ignore` is faster to write than diagnosing the actual mismatch.
antipattern.fix: ruff rule PGH003 forbids bare `# type: ignore`. Enforce `warn_unused_ignores = true` in mypy config so stale coded ignores fail too. Block PRs that introduce bare ignores.

antipattern.symptom: Code calls `isinstance(obj, MyProto)` where `MyProto(Protocol)` lacks `@runtime_checkable`. Runtime `TypeError: Instance and class checks can only be used with @runtime_checkable protocols`.
antipattern.root-cause: Protocol is opt-in for `isinstance` because runtime structural checks walk the MRO and signature list — too slow to enable by default. The error surfaces only at runtime, not at type-check.
antipattern.fix: Either add `@runtime_checkable` (accept the perf cost) OR replace `isinstance(x, Proto)` with structural duck-typing (`hasattr(x, 'send')`). Lint: `mypy --strict` does not catch this; add a custom grep rule.

antipattern.symptom: Same module mixes `Optional[X]`, `X | None`, and `Union[X, None]`. Ruff UP007 warnings accumulate; new contributors copy the closest example, perpetuating the mix.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple equivalent syntaxes exist for historical reasons; without a linter, drift is the default. The single-syntax convention requires enforcement, not preference.
antipattern.fix: Enable ruff rule UP007 and run `ruff check --fix --select UP007 .` as a one-shot migration. Add the rule to pre-commit. Result: only `X | None` remains in the codebase.
